lesson: c4-l03 / AI for Students with Hearing Impairments
title: AI for Students with Hearing Impairments

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: AI for students who cannot hear the lesson. [pause] Speech, turned into structured text.

scenes.name: The barrier
scenes.text: Teaching is largely a <emphasis>spoken</emphasis> activity. [pause] Explanations. Instructions. Discussions. Feedback. [pause] For a hearing-impaired student, every spoken word is a potential gap in access.

scenes.name: Real-time
scenes.text: Real-time transcription converts speech to text as it's spoken. [pause] Otter dot AI runs on a phone on the desk. The transcript appears on screen. [pause] An ephemeral medium becomes permanent and reviewable.

scenes.name: Tools
scenes.text: The toolkit. [pause] Otter dot AI for transcription. Built-in captions in Teams and Google Meet. [pause] Then ChatGPT or Claude — to process what was captured.

scenes.name: After class
scenes.text: After the lesson, raw transcripts become study material. [pause] Summaries. Structured notes. Vocabulary lists. Simpler explanations. [pause] Each prompt serves a different learning purpose.

scenes.name: Workflow
scenes.text: The most effective workflow has two stages. [pause] During the lesson — transcription captures everything. [pause] After the lesson — AI organises and adapts. Capture is fast. Processing is intelligent.

scenes.name: Improving
scenes.text: Small teacher habits make a big difference. [pause] Speak clearly. Moderate pace. Face the class. [pause] And repeat student questions before answering — so the full exchange lands in the transcript.

scenes.name: Limits
scenes.text: Limits to remember. [pause] Multi-speaker discussions reduce accuracy. Privacy laws govern recording. [pause] Transcripts are personal study tools — not documents to share widely.
